examples.title: Decibels (Sound)
examples.content: Sound loudness uses logarithmic scale. 10 dB = barely audible, 60 dB = normal conversation, 120 dB = painful. Normal scale would be useless.

examples.title: Earthquake Magnitude
examples.content: Richter scale is logarithmic. Magnitude 5 is 30× stronger than magnitude 4. Normal numbers would be confusing.

examples.title: Exponential Decay
examples.content: Radioactivity decays exponentially. On a normal graph: big drop then flat. On log scale: straight line! Shows you the decay rate clearly.
